input.headline: Ken Burns on his American Revolution documentary: ‘We won’t work on a more important film’

raw.headline: Ken Burns on his American Revolution documentary: ‘We won’t work on a more important film’

winner: Ken Burns says they won't work on a more important film, which is a shame because he’s clearly already made a revolution in the history of slow-motion.

candidates.text: Ken Burns said he won't make a more important film, which is reassuring because I was worried he was going to start a documentary on the invention of the sandwich.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising and specific comparison to a sandwich documentary adds clever humor while staying concise and relevant."}

candidates.text: Ken Burns said his next project will be about the history of socks—because apparently, the American Revolution wasn't dramatic enough.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist on a historical documentary subject, concise and specific with a touch of dry humor."}

candidates.text: Ken Burns said his next project won't be about the Civil War — he's already committed to a documentary called "The Revolution Will Not Be Televised... Unless It's on PBS.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "The joke is concise, specific, and surprising with a clever twist on Ken Burns' previous work and the title of a well-known song."}

candidates.text: Why did Ken Burns refuse to work on the American Revolution documentary? Because he was afraid of getting "patriotic" about it!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat specific and uses a play on Ken Burns' name, but the punchline feels forced and lacks genuine surprise or cleverness."}

candidates.text: Ken Burns says they won't work on a more important film, which is a shame because he’s clearly already made a revolution in the history of slow-motion.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Puns on 'revolution' and 'slow-motion' add clever surprise and specificity, with natural wordplay and brevity."}

candidates.text: Ken Burns says his next project will be a documentary about how he refused to make a more important film than the American Revolution.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist on the headline with clever self-referential humor."}

candidates.text: Ken Burns announced he's finally taking time off from his American Revolution documentary—because now that we've won, he's way too busy losing the next war.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat clever with the twist about 'losing the next war,' but it's a bit forced and lacks specificity. The reference to Ken Burns working on a more important film isn't clearly tied to the punchline, making it less surprising and slightly generic."}

candidates.text: Ken Burns announced his next project will be a documentary about American Revolution leftovers—he figures with all those uneaten apple pies and leftover fireworks, he’s got a goldmine.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat creative and specific, but the connection to 'leftovers' feels a bit forced and lacks the punch of a more surprising twist."}

candidates.text: Ken Burns is correct, as the production team is currently trying to film a documentary about a revolutionary toaster, but they have run out of bread and are forced to work on a less important film.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat creative with the toaster twist, but the reference to 'bread' feels forced and less surprising. The humor is light but lacks the sharpness and specificity that would make it stand out in a competition."}
